LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC AND BULLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA, LL'CS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT FAILS TO PRESENT CLEAR AUTHORITY THAT THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION SUBSUMES THE NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION AND LACK OF INFORMED CONSENT CAUSES OF ACTION. THE NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, AND UCL CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ALLEGED WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT TO SUPPORT THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL 20 DAYS LEAVE TO FILE AN ANSWER BEGINNING FROM TODAY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC AND BULLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS TO THE PRAYER FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE CONDUCT IS LIKELY TO REPEAT AND THE COURT TAKES THAT ALLEGATION AS TRUE FOR PURPOSES OF THIS MOTION. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE PRAYER FOR PREJUDGMENT INTEREST UNDER CC 3288. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES; IT IS A FACTUAL ISSUE THAT CANNOT YET BE DETERMINED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC AND BELLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR DAMAGES ARE NOT LIMITED TO CONDUCT THAT OCCURRED PRIOR TO THE ASSET PURCHASE THAT OCCURRED ON FEBRUARY 3, 2012. PLAINTIFF ALSO ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT IS LIABLE FOR ITS DIRECT MISCONDUCT THAT TOOK PLACE FROM FEBRUARY 2012 THROUGH JUNE OF 2012. THE COURT CANNOT GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION THAT DEFENDANT IS NOT LIABLE FOR ANY PRE-ASSET PURCHASE CONDUCT OF THE DEBTOR. CCP 437C(F)(1) PROVIDES THAT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION CAN ONLY BE GRANTED WHERE IT COMPLETELY DISPOSES OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication As To The First Amended Complaint Ntc Of Lodging Of Records Vol 1 Of 2 And 2 Of 2
Off Calendar Jul-03-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Loren Schwartz, Esq., 415.982.2100, for plaintiff Sydney Saidyan.
Geoffrey Moore, Esq., 714.241.8780, for defendants American Laser Skincare LLC and Bellus ALC of California, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC and BELLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication As To The First Amended Complaint is denied. Defendant failed to meet its initial burden of production. The motion for summary judgment is denied because plaintiff's claims for damages are not limited to conduct that occurred prior to the asset purchase that occurred on February 3, 2012. Plaintiff also alleges that defendant is liable for its direct misconduct that took place from February 2012 through June of 2012. The court cannot grant summary adjudication that defendant is not liable for any pre-asset purchase conduct of the debtor. CCP 437c(f)(1) provides that summary adjudication can only be granted where it completely disposes of the cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Set For Trial On Aug-04-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Continued To Nov-20-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. RYAN T. DUNN OF DUNN & PANAGOTACOS LLP, TEL. NO. 982-2100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CINDY A. SHAPIRO OF THE REBACK, MCANDREWS & MOORE LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 297-9900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC and BULLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA, LL'Cs DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Defendant fails to present clear authority that the negligence cause of action subsumes the negligent misrepresentation and lack of informed consent causes of action. The negligent misrepresentation, intentional misrepresentation, and UCL causes of action are alleged with sufficient particularity. The complaint alleges outrageous conduct to support the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The Court gave counsel 20 days leave to file an answer beginning from today. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. RYAN T. DUNN OF DUNN & PANAGOTACOS LLP, TEL. NO. 982-2100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CINDY A. SHAPIRO OF THE REBACK, MCANDREWS & MOORE LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 297-9900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC and BULLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. The motion to strike is denied as to the prayer for injunctive relief. The complaint alleges that the conduct is likely to repeat and the Court takes that allegation as true for purposes of this motion. The Court denies the motion to strike as to the prayer for prejudgment interest under CC 3288. The Court denies the motion to strike as to the prayer for attorney's fees; it is a factual issue that cannot yet be determined. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm
